action.reasoning: Observation: The custom pose-aware renderer fails because its rays miss the TripoSR scene box, and the latest ray diagnostic had a shape-indexing bug. Analysis: We need compare the built-in TripoSR camera rays against our custom spherical ray generation using the correct tensor shape before changing predict.py. Decision: Run a compact diagnostic that prints the actual get_spherical_cameras signature/source key lines and center-ray values/shapes for built-in vs custom rays.

signature (n_views: int, elevation_deg: float, camera_distance: float, fovy_deg: float, height: int, width: int)
source_n_lines 59
001: def get_spherical_cameras(
003:     elevation_deg: float,
004:     camera_distance: float,
009:     azimuth_deg = torch.linspace(0, 360.0, n_views + 1)[:n_views]
010:     elevation_deg = torch.full_like(azimuth_deg, elevation_deg)
011:     camera_distances = torch.full_like(elevation_deg, camera_distance)
013:     elevation = elevation_deg * math.pi / 180
014:     azimuth = azimuth_deg * math.pi / 180
017:     # right hand coordinate system, x back, y right, z up
018:     # elevation in (-90, 90), azimuth from +x to +y in (-180, 180)
019:     camera_positions = torch.stack(
021:             camera_distances * torch.cos(elevation) * torch.cos(azimuth),
022:             camera_distances * torch.cos(elevation) * torch.sin(azimuth),
023:             camera_distances * torch.sin(elevation),
028:     # default scene center at origin
029:     center = torch.zeros_like(camera_positions)
030:     # default camera up direction as +z
031:     up = torch.as_tensor([0, 0, 1], dtype=torch.float32)[None, :].repeat(n_views, 1)
033:     fovy = torch.full_like(elevation_deg, fovy_deg) * math.pi / 180
035:     lookat = F.normalize(center - camera_positions, dim=-1)
036:     right = F.normalize(torch.cross(lookat, up), dim=-1)
037:     up = F.normalize(torch.cross(right, lookat), dim=-1)
038:     c2w3x4 = torch.cat(
039:         [torch.stack([right, up, -lookat], dim=-1), camera_positions[:, :, None]],
042:     c2w = torch.cat([c2w3x4, torch.zeros_like(c2w3x4[:, :1])], dim=1)
043:     c2w[:, 3, 3] = 1.0
057:     rays_o, rays_d = get_rays(directions, c2w, keepdim=True, normalize=True)
059:     return rays_o, rays_d
